findings: PIDs are recorded per run under repo/.stampede/{RUN_ID}/pids (PIDS_DIR set/created; leaf PID discovered via process-tree walk from tmux pane PID).
In metaswarm mode, pidfiles are commander-###.pid; no separate squad PID tracking at launcher level.
Teardown only runs when explicitly invoked with --teardown; no global EXIT/INT trap in launcher, so interruption can leave sessions/pidfiles.
Teardown sends SIGTERM to PID from pidfile and removes pidfiles, then ends the tmux session; stale pidfile/PID reuse could terminate an unrelated process.
Duplicate-session cleanup ends tmux session without running teardown; if any agent process escapes tmux (or pidfiles persist), cleanup can be incomplete.
find_leaf_pid only recognizes copilot/gh copilot/claude/aider; custom --agent-cmd may lead to incorrect PID capture and ineffective teardown.
